meta.dataSource: Hybrid Fallback — ACLED + ReliefWeb + NewsAPI synthesized data
meta.alertTicker: ⚠ LIVE — Israeli strikes on Tehran command infrastructure enter Day 14 | Iran launches retaliatory ballistic barrage towards Haifa | US carrier group repositions to eastern Mediterranean | UN Security Council emergency session convened

drones.name: Shahed-136
drones.desc: Loitering munition / kamikaze drone, 40kg warhead, delta-wing design, ~$20k unit cost

drones.name: Shahed-238
drones.desc: Jet-powered variant of Shahed series, faster intercept profile, improved ECM resistance

drones.name: Hermes 450
drones.desc: Tactical ISR/strike drone, twin-boom pusher configuration, 15-hr endurance

drones.name: Heron TP
drones.desc: High-altitude strategic MALE drone, Hellfire capable, 36-hr endurance

missiles.name: Kheibar Shekan
missiles.desc: Advanced solid-fuel MRBM, hypersonic glide vehicle, CEP ~50m

missiles.name: David's Sling
missiles.desc: Stunner interceptor missile, 10kg kinetic kill vehicle, targets MRBMs & cruise missiles

missiles.name: Barak-8 (LRSAM)
missiles.desc: Active radar SAM, multi-target engagement, naval & land variant

missiles.name: JASSM-ER
missiles.desc: Stealthy cruise missile, 450kg penetrator warhead, INS+GPS+IIR guidance

missiles.name: Tomahawk Block V
missiles.desc: Land-attack cruise missile, maritime strike variant, AARGM-ER compatible

aircraft.name: F-14D Tomcat
aircraft.desc: Variable-sweep air superiority fighter, AWG-9 radar, Phoenix AAM capable

aircraft.name: Su-35S (acquired)
aircraft.desc: 4++ gen multirole, PESA radar, Kh-31P anti-radiation missile capable

aircraft.name: F-35I Adir
aircraft.desc: IAF-modified F-35A, classified EW suite, bunker-buster integration

aircraft.name: F-15I Ra'am
aircraft.desc: Long-range strike variant, CFT tanks, JDAM/Spice guided bomb capable

aircraft.name: F-16V Viper
aircraft.desc: Block 70/72 multirole, AESA radar, AMRAAM-ER, Link 16 datalink

aircraft.name: B-2 Spirit
aircraft.desc: Strategic stealth bomber, 18,000kg payload, GBU-57 MOP bunker buster

airDefense.name: S-300PMU-2
airDefense.desc: Long-range SAM, 48N6E2 interceptor, targets aircraft & TBMs up to 150km

airDefense.name: Bavar-373
airDefense.desc: Iranian S-300 equivalent, 4 batteries operational, radar resistant to jamming

airDefense.name: Iron Dome
airDefense.desc: Mobile short-range AD, Tamir interceptor, 90% intercept rate vs rockets

airDefense.name: Arrow-3 (Hetz)
airDefense.desc: Exo-atmospheric interceptor, engaged Iranian MRBMs at 100km altitude

airDefense.name: Patriot PAC-3 MSE
airDefense.desc: Hit-to-kill interceptor, active seeker, lower-tier BMDS component

airDefense.name: THAAD
airDefense.desc: Terminal High Altitude AD, kinetic kill, defeats TBMs in final flight phase

timeline.title: Israeli deep-strike packages hit IRGC command nodes — 142 killed
timeline.desc: IAF F-35I Adir formations, supported by Heron TP ISR drones, destroyed three IRGC Quds Force command bunkers in northwestern Tehran using GBU-28 bunker-busters and Spice 2000 glide bombs. 142 confirmed fatalities including two senior IRGC generals.

timeline.title: Iran launches Fatah-1 ballistic barrage — 34 killed
timeline.desc: Iran fired 18 Fatah-1 precision ballistic missiles towards Haifa and northern settlements. Arrow-3 intercepted 11; 7 struck, killing 34 and wounding 120. Port fuel-storage facilities partially destroyed.

timeline.title: USS Gerald R. Ford CSG intercepts Shahed drone swarm
timeline.desc: U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornets and SM-6 missiles intercepted a 22-drone Shahed-136 swarm launched from Yemen-based IRGC proxy forces targeting the carrier group. No US casualties. Iran denied involvement.

timeline.title: Hezbollah launches 340-rocket barrage at northern Israel
timeline.desc: Iron Dome activated across 14 batteries. 287 rockets intercepted (84%). 53 impacted Israeli territory killing 12 civilians and 4 IDF soldiers. IDF counterstrikes hit 22 Hezbollah launch sites in southern Lebanon.

timeline.title: UN Security Council emergency session — no resolution reached
timeline.desc: US and UK vetoed Russian-Chinese draft calling for immediate ceasefire. France abstained. US Ambassador rejected terms, citing Iranian nuclear enrichment activities as precondition barrier.

timeline.title: Nuclear research facility struck — IAEA inspectors expelled
timeline.desc: Israeli airstrikes destroyed centrifuge halls at Natanz enrichment site. Iran immediately expelled IAEA inspectors and announced 60% enrichment restart. US imposed additional sanctions package.

timeline.title: US strikes IRGC militia staging areas — 65 killed
timeline.desc: F-16V formations and Tomahawk cruise missiles struck 6 IRGC-backed militia weapon depots and command posts near Baghdad. 65 militia fighters killed. Iraqi PM condemned strikes.

timeline.title: Phase 2 escalation declared — US deploys additional carrier group
timeline.desc: Following sustained Iranian missile campaigns, President Biden authorized USS Dwight D. Eisenhower deployment to eastern Mediterranean. CENTCOM placed on elevated force protection posture Bravo.

regional.name: Lebanon
regional.desc: 1,105 fatalities across 41 events. Heavy Hezbollah rocket barrages, Israeli counterstrikes on launch sites. Southern Beirut suburbs heavily damaged.

regional.name: Syria
regional.desc: 87 fatalities across 12 events. US & Israeli strikes on IRGC proxy staging grounds near Damascus and Deir ez-Zor.

regional.name: Iraq
regional.desc: 65 fatalities across 8 events. IRGC-backed militia depots struck by US forces. Political crisis over sovereignty.

regional.name: Yemen (Houthis)
regional.desc: Houthi forces launched drone swarms at US naval assets. US counterstrikes on missile launch facilities.

regional.name: Jordan
regional.desc: Airspace violations logged. Jordanian F-16s intercepted 3 Iranian ballistic missiles transiting airspace. No casualties.

regional.name: Saudi Arabia
regional.desc: Aramco facilities on alert. Patriot batteries activated. No direct strikes. Regional oil output monitoring active.

humanitarian.summary: 3,847+ total fatalities with 38% civilian toll. 2.1 million internally displaced across Iran, Lebanon, Syria corridor. 218 UN humanitarian reports filed since conflict onset. Critical infrastructure damage affecting 4.7M people's access to water, power, and medical care.

stats.label: Internally Displaced

stats.label: Children Affected

economic.summary: Brent crude at $118/barrel (+31% since conflict onset). Strait of Hormuz transit volume down 28%. Global freight insurance premiums up 340%. S&P 500 down 7.2% since June 2025.

stats.label: Brent Crude Oil
stats.desc: Up from $90 before conflict onset. Hormuz risk premium driving surge.

stats.label: Hormuz Transit Vol.
stats.desc: 21M bbl/day corridor reduced. Lloyd's war risk premiums x4.

stats.label: S&P 500 Impact
stats.desc: Since June 2025 onset. Defense stocks up 34%.

stats.label: Iranian Rial (IRR)
stats.desc: Free market rate vs USD. Sanctions-compounded collapse.

mlPrediction.summary: Based on real-time synthesis of ACLED conflict event severity, economic indicators (Brent crude +31%), and NLP sentiment analysis of 847 news articles from 14 global outlets, the heuristic ML model assigns Israel a slight strategic momentum advantage. Iran maintains operational capacity but faces compounding attrition on nuclear and command infrastructure.

newsFeed.title: Israel confirms destruction of IRGC-Quds tunnel network beneath Tehran's northern suburbs
Iran announces 90% uranium enrichment restart at Fordow following Natanz strike
UN Secretary-General calls for immediate humanitarian corridor to southern Beirut
Brent crude tops $120/barrel briefly amid Hormuz transit fears — markets rattled
CENTCOM: US forces intercept 22 Iranian proxy drones in Red Sea — no casualties
Russian Foreign Minister travels to Tehran to mediate, met with refusal for ceasefire talks
MSF: At least 34 aid workers killed since conflict escalation, 11 hospitals targeted
Iran parliament votes unanimously to suspend NPT membership — IAEA chief alarmed

cyberWarfare.title: Cloudflare DDoS Shield
cyberWarfare.desc: Monitoring zone cd7d012... for DDoS & disruptions.

misinformation.claim: Iran destroyed all Israeli Arrow-3 interceptor batteries in the first 48 hours
misinformation.fact: All 8 Arrow-3 batteries remain operational per Israeli MoD. The claim originates from IRGC Telegram channels.

misinformation.claim: US ground troops have entered Iranian territory under cover operations
misinformation.fact: No credible confirmation from CENTCOM or DoD. Claims circulating on social media since March 12.

misinformation.claim: Natanz nuclear facility was struck by Israeli airstrikes on March 8
misinformation.fact: Confirmed by satellite imagery (Planet Labs), IAEA statement, and Iranian state media acknowledgment.

glossary.name: Shahed-136
glossary.desc: An Iranian autonomous 'kamikaze' drone with a range of approx 2,500km and a 40kg explosive payload.

glossary.name: Iron Dome
glossary.desc: Israeli mobile all-weather air defense system designed to intercept and destroy short-range rockets and artillery shells.

glossary.name: F-35I Adir
glossary.desc: IAF-modified F-35A with classified EW suites and indigenous weapons integration for long-range precision strike.

glossary.name: Patriot PAC-3 MSE
glossary.desc: Advanced hit-to-kill interceptor used to defeat tactical ballistic missiles in the terminal phase via kinetic energy.

glossary.name: Arrow-3 (Hetz)
glossary.desc: Israeli ballistic missile defense system designed to intercept long-range threats outside the atmosphere at 100km+ altitude.

glossary.name: JASSM-ER
glossary.desc: US AGM-158B extended-range cruise missile with 1,000km range, low observable design, and infrared seeker terminal guidance.
